Title: CRISPR/Cas9 mediated disruption of Inositol Pentakisphosphate 2-Kinase 1 (TaIPK1) reduces phytic acid and improves iron and zinc accumulation in wheat grains

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Inositol phosphate kinase (IPK) superfamily; Ins_P5_2-Kinase domain
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late/final step of phytic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Phosphorylates inositol 1,3,4,5,6-pentakisphosphate (IP5) to generate inositol hexakisphosphate (IP6, phytic acid)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phytic acid serves as a seed phosphorus storage compound but chelates metal ions and reduces iron and zinc bioavailability; iron and zinc are essential micronutrients for human nutrition
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: All three homeologs expressed in seeds at 14 and 21 DAA; TaIPK1.A showed the highest expression, especially at early grain filling (14 DAA), with reduced expression by 21 DAA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in developing seeds/grains during early grain filling (14 DAA), lower at 21 DAA
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing grain stage (14 vs 21 days after anthesis); greenhouse growth conditions
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CRISPR/Cas9 disruption of TaIPK1.A significantly reduces seed phytic acid and increases grain iron and zinc accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaIPK1.A was the most highly expressed TaIPK1 homeolog during early grain filling, making it the primary target for editing. 2. CRISPR/Cas9-induced indels in TaIPK1.A caused a strong reduction in mature seed phytic acid content in T2 wheat lines. 3. Reduced phytic acid was accompanied by approximately 1.5- to 2.1-fold higher iron and 1.6- to 1.9-fold higher zinc in grains. 4. Edited lines showed altered spike architecture, including reduced spike length and spikelet number, but increased grain weight and grain length in some lines.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Homoeolog isolation and sequencing; qRT-PCR expression analysis; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout with two sgRNAs; mutation screening by PCR/restriction assay and sequencing; phytic acid quantification; atomic absorption spectrometry for Fe and Zn; phenotypic analysis in T2 lines
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step; terminal determinant of phytate accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Rice Ubiquitin promoter (constitutive) driving Cas9 in the editing construct
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for CRISPR-based low-phytate, Fe/Zn-biofortified wheat; editing the highly expressed A-genome homeolog can improve micronutrient bioavailability and may be useful in breeding or gene-stacking strategies for biofortification
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced spike length and spikelet number in edited lines; however, no significant reduction in seed germination or plant height, and some lines showed increased grain weight and grain length
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Three wheat homeologs were isolated (TaIPK1.A, TaIPK1.B, TaIPK1.D), but functional editing focused on TaIPK1.A because of its stronger seed expression. The study frames IPK1 as a favorable downstream target compared with earlier phytic-acid-pathway enzymes to minimize pleiotropic effects.
